gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/247)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/247)
text: كَتَبْتُ إِلَيْهِ أَسْأَلُهُ عَنِ الْمَيِّتِ يَمُوتُ بِعَرَفَاتٍ يُدْفَنُ بِعَرَفَاتٍ أَوْ يُنْقَلُ إِلَى الْحَرَمِ فَأَيُّهُمَا أَفْضَلُ فَكَتَبَ يُحْمَلُ إِلَى الْحَرَمِ وَ يُدْفَنُ فَهُوَ أَفْضَلُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ali Bin Muhammad Bin Sheerat, from Ali Bin Suleyman who said,

‘I wrote to himasws asking himasws about the deceased having died at Arafaat, ‘Should he be buried at Arafaat or transferred to the Harram? So which of the two is superior?’ So heasws wrote: ‘Carry him to the Harram and bury him, for it is superior’.
en.sarwar: 14. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Afi ibn Muhammad ibn Shirah from Ali ibn Sulayman who has said the following: “I once wrote to him (in a muzmar manner), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and asked about a person who dies in ‘Arafat. Should he be buried in ‘Arafat or moved to al-Haram (the sacred area) and which one is more proper?’ He wrote back, ‘Moving to al-Haram (the sacred area) is more proper.
